economic_notes: Community Economic Development segment active; Business Enterprises segment includes production of clean renewable energy and provision of training and employment. Consolidated entities include South Bay Construction Ltd., OPCN Inc., and 5681490 Manitoba Ltd. (o/a South Indian Lake Rock Crushing Company).
notes: Audit is a Disclaimer of Opinion (not merely qualified) — auditors could not obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence due to scope limitations and classification issues. Financial statements for fiscal year ended March 31, 2020 were not completed until audit report dated June 28, 2023, causing non-compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act posting requirements. Amortization is recorded as a separate 'other item' below the operating surplus line rather than within program expenses. CMHC revenue was nil in 2020 (was $185,140 in 2019). ISC Funding Recovery of ($1,048,563) was recorded in 2019 comparative. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $7,614,941 + FNIH $2,396,719 + Province of Manitoba tobacco tax $300,067 + Manitoba Housing $47,567 = $10,359,294; Manitoba Hydro $806,622 may also be a transfer bringing total to $11,165,916 — exact classification uncertain due to disclaimer of opinion.
